title: pkg/retry does not detect `TOOMANYREQUESTS` when uploading manifest
body:
Running the following command:
```
skopeo --insecure-policy copy --retry-times 20 --preserve-digests --multi-arch index-only --format v2s2 --digestfile built_image_sha.txt docker://.../python@sha256:492b8322ceed4d33067b73e56145503a7d9a21c9f8e9d7768ebe9dbe8120586c docker://.../python:3.10.18-r0
```
output:
```
Getting image list signatures
Copying 0 images generated from 4 images in list
Writing manifest list to image destination
FATA[0000] Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(uploading manifest 3.10.18-r0 to ********************************************/python: toomanyrequests: Rate exceeded) 
```

Given that the target URL is Azure Container Registry, we are getting 429 rate limit exceeded but I expected the "--retry-times 20" to kick in and apparently it does not (it fails quickly)

Using latest skopeo:
skopeo version 1.19.0 commit: 7aa78df96b049bc9e36e10283ba08ceb9165041d

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

The current implementation of the “high-level” `--retry-times` mechanism only retries specific error kinds, to avoid making things worse in unexpected situations.

Does the server also serve a `Retry-After` header in this situation? If so, a ~clean solution would be to extend `c/image/docker.dockerClient.makeRequestToResolvedURL` to do “low-level” retries on 429 also when uploading in-memory data (currently it never does low-level retries on requests with a body). OTOH that would help only for manifest uploads, not for layer uploads.

Failing that, the high-level `c/common/pkg/retry` mechanism (controlled by `--retry-times`) _might_ be extended to recognize this error code. Philosophically, it’s… a bit unclear whether retrying on `registry/api/errorcode.ErrorCodeTooManyRequests` (“Returned when a client attempts to contact a service too many times`”) might count as “making things worse”.

Thanks for your prompt reply!
I think that the server returns Retry-After but even if it is omitted I think that when the user adds `--retry-times` they expect that a rate limited response from the server be retried, and I think that when using exponential backoff you won't be making things worse, the server expects for you to retry after the throttling settle down.
We are hitting this problem in a CI scenario that uses skopeo to copy multiple images in concurrent stages of the build, so we were surprised that `--retry-times` did not solve this issue

> I think that when using exponential backoff you won't be making things worse,

It’s difficult to say what _exactly_ the clients’ users were considering and what the servers’ operators meant, but I think that’s a very valid argument for presuming that the harm, if any, wouldn’t be catastrophic.

Reassigning to c/common to handle `errorcode.ErrorCodeTooManyRequests`, then.

… looking at the implementation, we are _already_ supposed to retry on most registry-reported errors. Would it be easy enough for you to capture the exact value + type of the `err` parameter of `IsErrorRetryable` (e.g. using a `%#v`)? I suspect this is an `errcode.Error` vs. `errcode.ErrorCode` situation, but that’s a guess. Alternatively, capturing the full HTTP response (headers + body) would help.
